scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of open Sea cages (100-120)m3 vol. - Gujarat
page_title: PMMSY: Establishment of open Sea cages (100-120)m3 vol. - Gujarat

details: The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Establishment of open Sea cages (100-120)m3 vol.", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.

Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.

Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.

Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits: Establishment of Open Sea Cages (100-120 m³ volume):
The estimated unit cost is ₹5,00,000/- for the general category against ₹(Rs.) 40% assistance, i.e., ₹2,00,000/- or 40% of the purchase (whichever is less).
60% contribution of the central government and 40% of the state government will be in the assistance received.

1 Sea-Cage unit under this scheme against the estimated unit cost of ₹ 5,00,00,000/- for women/SC/ST. 60% assistance for the category, i.e. assistance of ₹ 3,00,000/- or 60 % of the actual purchase, whichever is less, is available to the beneficiary.

60% contribution of the central government and 40% of the state government will be in the assistance received. The benefit will be available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.
60% contribution from Government of India and 40% contribution from State Governments is paid.
Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits:
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

faqs.question: What type of financial assistance is provided for establishing an open sea cage unit?
faqs.answer: For establishing an open sea cage unit of 100-120 m³ volume, financial assistance is given as a percentage of the unit cost, depending on the category of the beneficiary.

faqs.question: How is the contribution for financial assistance divided between governments?
faqs.answer: The assistance is shared, with 60% contributed by the Government of India and 40% by the Government of Gujarat.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on how many times a beneficiary can avail financial assistance for a sea cage unit?
faqs.answer: Yes, financial assistance for establishing a sea cage unit is available only once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Are there any specific targets for assistance under the sea cage component?
faqs.answer: Yes, benefits will be provided as per the target allocation decided by the Government of India.

faqs.question: What kind of support is given for cold storage and ice plant modernization?
faqs.answer: Assistance is provided to modernize cold storage and ice plants to keep fish in a healthy condition.

faqs.question: What facilities are supported for transporting and selling fish hygienically?
faqs.answer: Support is given for insulated vehicles, three-wheelers with ice boxes, e-rickshaws, and live fish vending centers to ensure fresh fish reaches consumers.

faqs.question: How do insulated vehicles and vending centers benefit consumers and sellers?
faqs.answer: They help consumers receive fresh and clean fish locally and enable sellers to get better returns.

faqs.question: Are there any exclusions related to the assistance?
faqs.answer: Yes, the benefit for a sea cage unit can only be claimed once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Where should beneficiaries register to apply for assistance?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries should register online through the iKhedut Gujarat portal.

faqs.question: How is the assistance amount disbursed to the beneficiary?
faqs.answer: After verification, the financial assistance is directly credited to the beneficiary’s bank account.

faqs.question: What additional documents are required for reserved categories?
faqs.answer: Caste certificate is required for SC/ST beneficiaries, and proof of gender is needed for women beneficiaries claiming higher assistance.

faqs.question: What documents are needed related to fishing activities?
faqs.answer: The applicant must provide a fishing boat registration certificate from Real Craft software and a valid fishing license.
